subject: fix(timers): apply TIMER_LEAKS pattern to glow-props itself
body:
src/pwa.js + src/markdown.js had raw setTimeout/setInterval/addEventListener calls without cleanup paths. Both modules sit in Vite's module graph, so HMR re-evaluation was layering fresh listeners on top of the old ones every cycle: a new SW update interval, a new beforeinstallprompt listener, a new appinstalled listener, a new 1s install-fallback timer, and unbounded toast/copy-feedback timers.

src/pwa.js:
- swUpdateIntervalId, manualInstallTimeoutId, toastTimeouts[], beforeInstallPromptListener, appInstalledListener, domContentLoadedListener tracked at module scope
- nested toast exit/remove timeouts pushed into the same array (variant 1)
- window-level beforeinstallprompt + appinstalled wrapped behind window.__pwaModuleAttached HMR guard (variant 5)
- import.meta.hot.dispose() drains every tracked id, removes every tracked listener, closes any open install modal, resets the HMR flag (variant 4)

src/markdown.js:
- feedbackTimeouts[] tracks the 1500ms button-text-reset timers in copyMarkdown + copyCodeBlock
- import.meta.hot.dispose() clears them

Closes the self-compliance gap surfaced by the 2026-04-25 fleet re-validation. TODO Cross-Cutting Gaps item 17 updated; glow-props moves from "trailing items" to "fully clean".

Tags: pwa, timers, hmr, cleanup, pattern-compliance
